page: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g is a virtual server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an AMD x86_64 processor architecture configured with 96 vCPUs and 96 physical cores, operating at 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model, which distributes physical processor resources among multiple tenants. It does not include integrated graphics processing units (GPUs). Storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, requiring external network storage solutions for data-intensive applications. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Due to the shared CPU architecture, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for high-core-count workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for general-purpose workloads, distributed testing environments, large-scale web serving, and memory-intensive applications that can tolerate shared CPU resource scheduling.
description: Vultr vx1-g-96c-384g is a General Purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, powered by an AMD x86_64 processor. It features 96 vCPUs and 96 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include dedicated GPU hardware and is configured with 1 GB of network-attached storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address. Because it utilizes shared CPU resources, this server provides a cost-effective option for high-density deployments that require substantial memory and core counts without the premium of dedicated CPU threads. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, large-scale web serving, and distributed testing environments that can accommodate shared resource scheduling.
